agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to replace 1965 zoning bylaw with 2026 version
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to repeal Burnaby's current Zoning Bylaw (from 1965) and replace it with a new Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026. This proposed change would also introduce new Development Permit Area Guidelines and reassign some properties to different zoning districts. If approved, the new bylaw would update the rules for what can be built and where, affecting property owners, developers, and businesses across the city.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on how the new zoning rules would affect your property or business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority Year 2 operating funding request
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) request for Year 2 operating funding. This is a proposal to provide money to help the BHA run its housing programs for another year. The decision has not been made yet — council will vote on whether to approve this funding at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 2026 property tax rates for Burnaby businesses
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the 2026 property tax rates for municipal, regional, and Business Improvement Area (BIA) purposes. This decision would set the tax rates that all commercial and residential property owners in Burnaby will pay next year. A BIA is a local area where businesses pay an extra levy to fund improvements and marketing for that specific commercial district. If approved, these rates will determine how much your business property taxes increase or change in 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to appoint an Approving Officer
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to appoint an Approving Officer. An Approving Officer is a person who has the authority to approve plans for new subdivisions (dividing land into smaller lots) and road closures. This is a routine administrative appointment, but it affects anyone who wants to subdivide land or close a road in Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on the appointment at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you at this time. Watch for the council decision if you plan to subdivide land or close a road in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on retaining wall height variance at 8012 11th Ave
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property at 8012 11th Avenue. A DVP is a special permission that lets a property owner break certain rules in the zoning bylaw. In this case, the owner wants to build retaining walls that are taller than what the current rules allow. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider the request at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own or develop property near 8012 11th Avenue, watch for the council decision — it could set a precedent for similar height variances in your area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to award insurance contracts for property and liability programs
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding new contracts for the city's annual property and liability insurance programs. This is a routine renewal of insurance coverage that protects city assets and operations. The decision is on the agenda for the next council meeting and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve Heritage Commission members attending 2026 BC conference
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to allow members of Burnaby's Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC, from May 4-6, 2026. This is a request for approval to send commission members to the conference, not a final decision yet.

key_dates.label: 2026 Heritage BC Conference in Squamish takes place

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
2026 HERITAGE BC CONFERENCE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for members of the Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC from May 4-6, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract award for networking software licenses
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. If approved, this would authorize the city to purchase the software needed to run its computer networks. This is a routine procurement decision that keeps city technology systems working.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for new civic facility furniture
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply, delivery, and installation of office and facility furniture for a new civic facility. If approved, this contract would cover the fit-out, furnishings, and equipment needed to complete the building. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility furniture.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for external audit services
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed change to the contract for the city's external audit services. This is not a new law or bylaw, but a decision about who will audit the city's financial statements and how much they will be paid. If approved, the change would affect the company that performs the annual audit of the city's finances. This is a routine administrative matter that ensures the city's books are checked by an independent firm.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for External Audit Services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for geotechnical work at Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for geotechnical consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. Geotechnical consultants study soil and ground conditions to ensure buildings are safe and stable. If approved, this change would adjust the scope or cost of the consultant's work on the project. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you now — watch for the council decision if you are a contractor or supplier on this project.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - BURNABY LAKE RECREATION COMPLEX GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Spring 2026 community grant recipients
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving the recommended recipients for the Spring 2026 community grant intake. This decision will determine which local organizations and projects receive funding from the city. The grants are part of the city's regular program to support community groups, non-profits, and events that benefit Burnaby residents. If approved, the selected applicants will receive their grant money to carry out their proposed activities. The council meeting agenda lists this item for discussion and a final vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Mid-year 2026 fee changes to Burnaby Fees Bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider mid-year 2026 amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would update certain fee schedules — meaning the fees the city charges for various permits, licences, and services could go up or down. The decision has not been made yet; council is set to vote on the proposed changes at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, the new fees could affect your business licence or permit costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and Framework up for Council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Tenant Protection Framework and a new Tenant Protection Bylaw. If approved, this bylaw would create new rules for landlords, including rules about when and how they can end a tenancy, renovate a unit, or increase rent. The goal is to give renters more stability and prevent 'renovictions' (when a landlord evicts a tenant to do major renovations, often at a much higher rent). The proposed bylaw would also require landlords to get a permit before doing renovations that force a tenant to move out. Council will consider this at an upcoming meeting. It has not been passed yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Landlords and property managers in Burnaby should read the proposed bylaw and attend the council meeting or public hearing to have your say.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and associated bylaw amendments.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on Southeast Bikeway Upgrade improvements
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. If approved, this project would add new bike lanes, crossings, and safety upgrades along the route. The decision is not final yet — Council will discuss and vote on the plan at an upcoming meeting. Business owners near the bikeway should be aware that construction could affect traffic, parking, and customer access in the area.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, prepare for possible construction impacts on your business access and parking.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT - PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update design standards for safer intersections
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to update the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards. The change would require new street designs to include 'protected intersection geometry' — a safer layout that separates pedestrians and cyclists from turning vehicles. If approved, this would apply to new developments and road projects in the Town Centre area. The goal is to make walking and biking safer for people of all ages and abilities.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your development or construction project in Burnaby's Town Centre.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote April 28: Exempt Brentwood Park from SSMUH via building bylaw change
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will vote on a motion to ask the province to exempt Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from provincial Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing (SSMUH) rules. Staff recommend against a city-wide building bylaw amendment requiring compliance with Restrictive Covenants at building permit stage, citing added costs, process delays, and impacts on residential, commercial, and industrial properties city-wide.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the April 28, 2026 council meeting or submit comments if you own property in Brentwood Park with a Restrictive Covenant.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting on Brentwood Park SSMUH exemption

agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION - RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY BUILDING SCHEMES
Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.

attachments.title: Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schem

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive building permit activity report for March 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from March 1 to March 31, 2026. This is an information item only — no decision or bylaw is being voted on. The report gives Council a snapshot of construction activity in the city, including the number and types of permits issued. For business owners, this is a useful way to track development trends in your area, but there is no proposed rule change or new requirement to act on.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Heritage designation for 3789 Royal Oak Avenue, Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a report that recommends formally recognizing 3789 Royal Oak Avenue as a heritage resource. The report includes an 'as-found' record (a detailed description of the property's current condition) and a 'Statement of Significance' (a document explaining why the property is historically important). If Council approves this, the property could be added to the municipal heritage register, which may affect future renovations, demolitions, or redevelopment plans. This is a proposed step — no final decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if you own or manage this property, contact Burnaby's planning department to understand how heritage designation could affect your plans.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
CHC - AS-FOUND REPORT AND STATEMENT OF SIGNIFICANCE FOR HERITAGE RESOURCES AT 3789 ROYAL OAK AVENUE
Purpose: To provide Council an as-found report and Statement of Significance for heritage resources located at 7389 Royal Oak Avenue for information.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby solid waste and recycling 2025 annual report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the City of Burnaby's 2025 annual report on solid waste and recycling collection. This report summarizes how the city's garbage, recycling, and organics collection system performed over the past year. It is a review of current operations, not a proposed new bylaw or fee change. No new rules or costs are being voted on at this time.
agenda_briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive update on proposed food sustainability measures in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an operational update on proposed food sustainability measures. This update will outline current and planned actions the City is considering to improve local food systems, reduce food waste, and support community food security. No decisions have been made yet — this is a report to inform Council before any future votes on specific bylaws or programs.
agenda_briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Agriculture
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5
EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Safety improvements at Central Boulevard and Metropolis parkade intersection
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. The proposal aims to make the area safer for drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. A decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming item on the council agenda for discussion and a potential vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6
TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on rezoning for hotel, more homes at 3900 Grand Promenade
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning amendment for four properties near the Carrigan site. The developer wants to replace some planned office space with a hotel and restaurants, and add 362 more market strata (condo) units. This would make Tower 1B seven storeys taller and Tower 1A four storeys taller than previously approved. The developer also proposes to shift some required community benefits (called 'inclusionary requirements') from this phase to the Carrigan site instead. This is a proposed change to a rezoning that was already approved in 2024 — it has not been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 12-storey mixed-use development at 6535 Kingsway
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would allow a 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway. The plan includes 290 residential units, ground-floor commercial space facing Kingsway, and underground parking. This proposal is consistent with the city's Official Community Plan and the Edmonds Community Plan. If approved, the project would bring new housing and retail to the area.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw change that would allow a five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. The project follows the city's 1998 Official Community Plan (the long-term vision for land use) and approved transition rules. If approved, the rezoning would permit construction of an enclosed storage facility on the site.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot program bylaw amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend Burnaby's Street and Traffic Bylaw to allow a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, the pilot would set new rules for where and how these devices can be used on city streets and paths. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming item on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new rules for e-bikes and e-scooters could affect your business operations or deliveries.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot in parks
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 would create new rules for where and how these devices can be used. This is not yet law — the decision is still upcoming.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this pilot could affect where you can ride e-bikes and e-scooters in Burnaby parks.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment to enforce e-bike and e-scooter rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This change would allow the city to issue tickets and fines for violations related to e-bikes and electric kick scooters as part of a pilot program. If approved, the city would have the power to enforce rules like where these devices can be ridden and how fast they can go. The amendment updates an existing 2009 bylaw to include these new vehicle types. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if passed, this could affect how you operate a business that rents or sells e-bikes or e-scooters.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick scooter programs)
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 property tax rates bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would set the 2026 tax rates for all land and buildings (called 'improvements') in Burnaby. This bylaw is required under sections 197 and 216 of BC's Community Charter, which gives municipalities the power to set property tax rates each year. If approved, this bylaw will determine how much property tax you pay in 2026 based on the assessed value of your land and buildings.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this bylaw will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter
